main_topic_title: The Gut-Brain Axis: Overcoming Brain Fog and Mood Instability Through Nutritional Psychiatry
science_backing: The gut-brain axis connects the digestive tract directly to cognitive and emotional centers. Approximately 90% of the body's serotonin is produced in the gut. A lack of dietary fiber disrupts the intestinal microbiota, driving systemic inflammation that manifests as brain fog and chronic fatigue. Feeding the microbiome with prebiotic whole foods directly regulates neurotransmitters, reducing inflammation to support mental clarity and mood stability.
hooks: Brain fog and a bad mood aren't just in your head—they start in your gut.
Can your diet cure brain fog and stabilize your mood? Nutritional psychiatry says yes.
Why 90% of your happiness hormone is made in your digestive tract, and how to feed it for mental clarity.
